message.content: {"cases":[{"input_lines":["5","[[0, 1, 0.0], [1, 2, 1e-12], [2, 3, 0.5], [3, 4, 1e-11], [5, 0, 2.0]]","[1.234567, 1.234567, 1.234567, 1.234567, 1.234567]"],"focus":"Identical boundary conditions (range ~0) causing domain-mapping zeros; tiny capacities near threshold and one invalid edge to test filtering and capacity-threshold logic."},{"input_lines":["1","[]","[2.5]"],"focus":"Single-node network with no edges to exercise degree clamping, single-coefficient derivative behavior, and empty/degenerate transforms."},{"input_lines":["2","[[0, 1, 10.0], [1, 0, 5e-11]]","[1000.0, -1000.0]"],"focus":"Very small node count (2) forces chebyshev degree clamp > nodes, extreme opposing boundary values to test normalization/interpolation clamping, and a negligible-capacity edge to trigger threshold skipping."}]}
